article_title: Obligations of deployers of high-risk AI systems
obligation_text: Deployers shall monitor the operation of the high-risk AI system on the basis of the instructions for use and, where relevant, inform providers. Where deployers have reason to consider that the use of the high-risk AI system in accordance with the instructions may result in that AI system presenting a risk, they shall, without undue delay, inform the provider or distributor and the relevant market surveillance authority and shall suspend the use of that system.
scoring_text: Skill in AI system operational monitoring, including performance monitoring against instructions for use, risk signal identification, provider notification procedures, market surveillance authority reporting, and system suspension decision-making.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in AI system operational monitoring, including performance monitoring against instructions for use, risk signal identification, provider notification procedures, market surveillance authority reporting, and system suspension decision-making.
reference_document_element_description: Establish post-deployment AI monitoring standards, including performance threshold definitions, drift detection protocols, incident reporting procedures, and remediation timelines.
comments: EU AI Act Art. 26(5) (obligation) superset of WIDAI DG domain. DG-T-007 is the strongest match (STS: 0.600, cosine: 0.678). Secondary: RM-T-005 (RM, cosine: 0.675), AI-S-010 (AI, cosine: 0.647).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the regulatory requirement) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.0/10 (raw 0.600). Shared vocabulary: [ai, monitoring, performance, procedures, reporting]. EU AI Act competency implication terms [against, authority, decision, identification, instructions, making] vs WIDAI terms [definitions, deployment, detection, drift, establish, incident] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.894 (P=0.891, R=0.896). Balanced P/R — the 27-word competency implication and 18-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs (mean ~0.007) because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (97.9%). Semantic tension between "Skill in AI system operational monitoring, including performance monitoring agai" and "Establish post-deployment AI monitoring standards, including performance thresho". Despite STS-based 'Superset of' classification, these concepts may operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.678 > STS 0.600 (gap +0.079). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ai, monitoring, performance, procedures, reporting] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 18.4%. Shared tokens: [ai, monitoring, performance, procedures, reporting]. EU AI Act-only: [against, authority, decision, identification, instructions, making]. WIDAI-only: [definitions, deployment, detection, drift, establish, incident].
